# Development Guidelines for BWS Website

## Important Development Rules

### 1. Always Modify Templates, Never Generated Files
**CRITICAL**: When fixing issues with HTML or content, ALWAYS modify the source template files in the `src/` directory (e.g., `src/components/IndexMainContent.astro`, `src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro`), NEVER modify the generated HTML files in `_site/` or built output.

- ✅ CORRECT: Edit `src/components/IndexMainContent.astro` to fix image attributes
- ❌ WRONG: Edit `_site/index.html` directly
- ❌ WRONG: Edit any file in the `_site/` directory

The build process will regenerate all HTML files from the templates. Any direct edits to generated files will be lost on the next build.

### 2. Understanding Auto-Generated vs. Manual Content

**CRITICAL**: The website has both manual and auto-generated content. You must know which is which.

#### Auto-Generated Content (DO NOT EDIT MANUALLY)

These files are created and updated by automation scripts:

**Article Pages:**
- `src/pages/articles/*.astro` - Generated by `scripts/generate-articles.js`
- `src/components/articles/*MainContent.astro` - Generated by `scripts/generate-articles.js`
- `src/data/articles.ts` - Updated by `scripts/generate-articles.js`
- `public/assets/images/articles/` - Downloaded by `scripts/generate-articles.js`

**Success Stories:**
- `src/data/successStories.ts` - Updated by `scripts/fetch-success-stories.js`
- `public/assets/images/success-stories/` - Downloaded by `scripts/fetch-success-stories.js`

**Partnership News:**
- `src/data/news.ts` - Updated by `scripts/fetch-twitter-partnerships.js`
- `public/assets/images/news/` - Downloaded by `scripts/fetch-twitter-partnerships.js`

**Documentation Index:**
- `scripts/data/docs-index.json` - Updated by `scripts/index-docs-site.js`

**State Tracking Files:**
- `scripts/data/processed-article-tweets.json`
- `scripts/data/processed-success-story-tweets.json`
- `scripts/data/processed-tweets.json`

⚠️ **If you edit these files manually, your changes will be overwritten by the next automation run.**

#### Manual Content (EDIT FREELY)

These files should be edited manually:

**Core Pages:**
- `src/pages/index.astro`, `about.astro`, `industries.astro`, etc.
- `src/pages/marketplace/*.astro`
- `src/pages/industry-content/*.astro`

**Components:**
- `src/components/Navigation.astro`
- `src/components/Footer.astro`
- `src/components/*MainContent.astro` (except articles/)
- `src/components/home/*.astro`

**Data Files:**
- `src/data/solutions.ts`
- `src/data/quarterContent.ts`
- `src/data/quarterLearnings.ts`

**Static Assets:**
- `public/styles.css`
- `public/assets/images/` (except articles/, success-stories/, news/)

### 3. Modifying Auto-Generated Content

If you need to change auto-generated content, you have two options:

**Option 1: Modify the Generation Script**
1. Edit the relevant script in `scripts/`:
   - `scripts/generate-articles.js` - For article generation
   - `scripts/fetch-success-stories.js` - For success stories
   - `scripts/fetch-twitter-partnerships.js` - For partnership news
2. Test the script locally with environment variables set
3. Verify the output matches expectations
4. Commit the script changes
5. Next automation run will use new logic

**Option 2: Use Manual Override**
1. For success stories, edit `scripts/data/manual-success-stories.json`
2. Add your manual entry with required fields
3. Script will process manual entries on next run

**Never:**
- ❌ Edit generated files directly
- ❌ Commit changes to auto-generated files
- ❌ Delete state tracking files without resetting them properly

### 4. CSS Organization

All CSS styles are consolidated in `/public/styles.css`. This unified file:
- Combines styles from multiple original Webflow CSS files
- Removes conflicting and redundant definitions
- Is served directly from the root path as `/styles.css`
- Includes custom classes for dynamic content (`.article-image-clickable`, `.success-story-image-clickable`)

**When adding new styles:**
- Add to `/public/styles.css` only
- Follow existing naming conventions
- Group related styles together
- Add comments for complex or non-obvious styles
- Test on all breakpoints (mobile, tablet, desktop)

### 5. TypeScript Data Files

All content data uses TypeScript interfaces for type safety.

#### Working with Data Files

**For Manual Content:**
```typescript
// Edit src/data/solutions.ts, quarterContent.ts, etc.
export const solutions: Solution[] = [
  {
    // Add your data with proper typing
  }
];
```

**For Auto-Generated Content (read-only):**
```typescript
// DO NOT EDIT src/data/articles.ts, successStories.ts, news.ts
// These are updated by automation scripts
import { articles } from '../data/articles';  // Use in components
import { successStories } from '../data/successStories';
import { news } from '../data/news';
```

#### Type Safety Best Practices
- Always use defined interfaces
- Don't use `any` type
- Ensure all required fields are present
- Use optional fields (`?`) appropriately
- Add new interfaces when needed

### 6. Code Standards

#### Astro Components
- Use `.astro` files for all page templates and components
- Keep components small and focused on a single responsibility
- Use semantic HTML elements
- Maintain consistent naming conventions
- Frontmatter (---) at the top for imports and logic
- Template section for HTML markup
- Inline `<script>` and `<style>` only when necessary

**Example Structure:**
```astro
---
// Imports
import BaseLayout from '../layouts/BaseLayout.astro';

// Logic
const data = await fetchData();
---

<!-- Template -->
<BaseLayout title="Page Title">
  <main>
    <!-- Content -->
  </main>
</BaseLayout>

<script>
  // Client-side JavaScript if needed
</script>
```

#### File Naming

**Pages:**
- Use kebab-case: `index.astro`, `about.astro`, `contact-us.astro`
- Match URL structure: `src/pages/about.astro` → `/about.html`

**Components:**
- Use PascalCase for main content: `IndexMainContent.astro`
- Use kebab-case for utilities: `news-item.astro`
- Descriptive names: `MarketplaceAnnouncementCarousel.astro`

**Data Files:**
- Use camelCase: `articles.ts`, `successStories.ts`, `quarterContent.ts`

**Scripts:**
- Use kebab-case: `generate-articles.js`, `fetch-success-stories.js`

#### Image Assets

**Manual Images:**
- Store in `/public/assets/images/[category]/`
- Use descriptive file names without spaces or special characters
- Optimize images before adding to repository
- Reference with absolute paths from root: `/assets/images/...`

**Auto-Generated Images:**
- Never commit these manually
- Scripts handle download and naming
- Located in `/public/assets/images/articles/`, `success-stories/`, `news/`

### 7. Development Workflow

#### Before Making Changes
1. Pull latest changes from master branch
2. Review related documentation in `/docs/`
3. Check if content is auto-generated or manual
4. Check existing patterns in similar files
5. Ensure you have latest automation scripts

#### During Development
1. Work in feature branches (except for automation commits)
2. Test changes locally with development server (`npm run dev`)
3. Ensure no console errors or warnings
4. Validate HTML and CSS syntax
5. Test responsive behavior (mobile, tablet, desktop)
6. Check browser compatibility

#### After Making Changes
1. Build the site to verify no build errors (`npm run build`)
2. Run relevant tests (`cd tests && npm test`)
3. Update documentation if needed
4. Commit with clear, descriptive messages
5. Push to remote and create PR if needed
6. Monitor CI/CD workflow in GitHub Actions

### 8. Working with Automation Scripts

#### Testing Scripts Locally

**Set Environment Variables:**
```bash
export TWITTER_BEARER_TOKEN="your_token_here"
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="your_key_here"
```

**Run Scripts:**
```bash
# Generate articles
node scripts/generate-articles.js

# Fetch success stories
node scripts/fetch-success-stories.js

# Fetch partnership news
node scripts/fetch-twitter-partnerships.js

# Index documentation
node scripts/index-docs-site.js
```

**Verify Output:**
- Check generated files in `src/data/`, `src/pages/articles/`, `src/components/articles/`
- Check downloaded images in `public/assets/images/`
- Check state tracking files in `scripts/data/`
- Run `npm run build` to verify site still builds

#### Resetting Automation State

**CAUTION:** Only reset if you know what you're doing.

```bash
# Backup first
cp scripts/data/processed-article-tweets.json scripts/data/processed-article-tweets.json.backup

# Reset to empty state
echo '{"processedTweetIds":[],"lastCheck":null,"lastSuccess":null,"failureCount":0}' > scripts/data/processed-article-tweets.json
```

Repeat for other state files as needed.

### 9. Common Patterns

#### Adding a New Page (Manual Content)
1. Create new file in `src/pages/`: `src/pages/new-page.astro`
2. Create corresponding content component: `src/components/NewPageMainContent.astro`
3. Import and use `BaseLayout` component
4. Add navigation entry if needed in `Navigation.astro`
5. Test routing and navigation
6. Add tests in `tests/` directory

**Example:**
```astro
---
// src/pages/new-page.astro
import BaseLayout from '../layouts/BaseLayout.astro';
import Navigation from '../components/Navigation.astro';
import NewPageMainContent from '../components/NewPageMainContent.astro';
---

<BaseLayout title="New Page Title" description="SEO description">
  <Navigation />
  <NewPageMainContent />
</BaseLayout>
```

#### Modifying Navigation
1. Edit `src/components/Navigation.astro`
2. Update links in the appropriate section
3. Ensure all links are working
4. Test mobile and desktop views
5. Check active state highlighting

#### Adding a Manual Success Story
1. Edit `scripts/data/manual-success-stories.json`
2. Add entry with all required fields:
```json
{
  "manualStories": [
    {
      "tweetId": "1234567890",
      "tweetUrl": "https://x.com/BWSCommunity/status/1234567890",
      "product": "Blockchain Badges",
      "partnerName": "Acme Corp",
      "description": "Partnership description..."
    }
  ]
}
```
3. Run script locally or wait for daily automation
4. Verify output in `src/data/successStories.ts`

#### Creating a New Component
1. Create file in `src/components/`: `MyComponent.astro`
2. Define props interface if needed
3. Implement component logic and template
4. Import and use in parent components
5. Test component in isolation
6. Add to documentation if it's reusable

**Example:**
```astro
---
// src/components/MyComponent.astro
interface Props {
  title: string;
  description?: string;
}

const { title, description } = Astro.props;
---

<div class="my-component">
  <h2>{title}</h2>
  {description && <p>{description}</p>}
</div>
```

#### Adding Client-Side Interactivity
1. Add `<script>` tag in component with `is:inline` attribute
2. Use `document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', ...)` for initialization
3. Keep JavaScript minimal and focused
4. Consider progressive enhancement
5. Test with JavaScript disabled

**Example:**
```astro
<div class="interactive-element" data-value="123">
  Click me
</div>

<script is:inline>
  if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
    window.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
      const element = document.querySelector('.interactive-element');
      element.addEventListener('click', function() {
        console.log(this.getAttribute('data-value'));
      });
    });
  }
</script>
```

### 10. Testing Guidelines

#### Local Testing
```bash
# Start dev server
npm run dev

# Build production site
npm run build

# Run all tests
cd tests && npm test

# Run specific test suite
cd tests && npm run test:smoke
cd tests && npm run test:e2e

# Run tests in UI mode
cd tests && npm run test:ui
```

#### What to Test
- All new pages load correctly
- Navigation links work
- Responsive behavior on all screen sizes
- Images load properly
- Forms submit correctly
- Client-side JavaScript functions work
- No console errors or warnings
- Accessibility (WCAG compliance)

#### Writing New Tests
1. Add test file in appropriate `tests/` subdirectory
2. Use Page Object Model pattern
3. Test user-facing behavior, not implementation
4. Include positive and negative test cases
5. Use descriptive test names
6. Clean up after tests (if needed)

### 11. Troubleshooting

#### Build Failures
- Check for syntax errors in Astro files
- Verify all imports are correct
- Ensure no missing dependencies
- Check for TypeScript type errors
- Clear `_site/` directory and rebuild: `npm run prebuild && npm run build`
- Check build logs for specific error messages

#### Style Issues
- All styles should be in `/public/styles.css`
- Check for CSS specificity conflicts
- Use browser DevTools to debug
- Verify styles aren't being overridden
- Check responsive breakpoints
- Validate CSS syntax

#### Image Loading Issues
- Verify image paths are absolute from root
- Check file names don't contain spaces or special characters
- Ensure images exist in `/public/assets/images/`
- Verify image file extensions are correct
- Check for case sensitivity in file names
- Verify image optimization didn't corrupt files

#### Auto-Generated Content Issues
- Check workflow logs in GitHub Actions
- Verify API secrets are valid (TWITTER_BEARER_TOKEN, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
- Check state tracking files in `scripts/data/`
- Run script locally to reproduce issue
- Review auto-created fix branches and PRs
- Check for rate limiting on APIs

#### Test Failures
- Check test output for specific error
- Run failing test in UI mode: `cd tests && npm run test:ui`
- Verify test environment is properly set up
- Check if test needs updated selectors
- Review test artifacts and screenshots
- Ensure preview server is running for tests

### 12. Git Workflow

#### Branching Strategy
- `master` - Production branch, auto-deploys to www.bws.ninja
- `feature/*` - Feature branches for new development
- `fix/*` - Bug fix branches
- Auto-generated branches: `fix/article-generation-failure-*`, etc.

#### Commit Messages
- Use clear, descriptive messages
- Start with imperative verb: "Add", "Fix", "Update", "Remove"
- Reference issue numbers when applicable
- Automation commits include Claude Code attribution

**Good Examples:**
- `Add success story lightbox functionality`
- `Fix navigation mobile menu overflow`
- `Update article generation to include SEO descriptions`
- `Remove deprecated partnership fetch logic`

**Bad Examples:**
- `fix bug`
- `updates`
- `WIP`

#### Pull Request Guidelines
- Create PR for significant changes
- Include description of what changed and why
- Reference related issues
- Ensure CI tests pass
- Request review from team members
- Don't merge failing PRs

### 13. Performance Best Practices

#### Images
- Optimize images before committing (use tools like ImageOptim, TinyPNG)
- Use appropriate formats (JPEG for photos, PNG for graphics)
- Provide appropriate image sizes
- Use lazy loading for below-fold images
- Automation scripts handle optimization for downloaded images

#### CSS
- Avoid inline styles when possible
- Use existing classes from `/public/styles.css`
- Minimize CSS specificity
- Group related styles together
- Remove unused styles

#### JavaScript
- Keep client-side JavaScript minimal
- Use progressive enhancement
- Defer non-critical scripts
- Avoid large dependencies
- Test performance with Lighthouse

#### Build Time
- Don't commit unnecessary files to `_site/`
- Keep automation scripts efficient
- Cache dependencies when possible
- Run builds locally before pushing

### 14. Security Best Practices

#### Secrets Management
- Never commit API keys, tokens, or credentials
- Use GitHub Secrets for workflow credentials
- Use `.env` file locally (gitignored)
- Rotate API keys regularly
- Use minimal required permissions

#### Content Security
- Validate all user inputs (if any)
- Sanitize auto-generated content
- Use HTTPS for all resources
- Keep dependencies updated
- Monitor for security vulnerabilities

#### Code Review
- Review auto-generated content before publishing
- Check for XSS vulnerabilities in dynamic content
- Verify external links are safe
- Test authentication flows (if added)
- Follow principle of least privilege
